article_title: ARPA-H Awards $177 Million to Five Teams Racing to Bioprint Transplantable Human Organs Within Five Years

content_preview.title: ARPA-H Awards $177 Million to Five Teams Racing to Bioprint Transplantable Human Organs Within Five Years
content_preview.summary: ARPA-H has awarded up to $176.8 million to five teams building 3D-bioprinted kidneys and livers designed to work without immunosuppressive drugs, targeting first-in-human trials within five years.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## The Program

The Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health (ARPA-H) announced on January 12 that it has selected five research teams to receive awards totaling up to [$176.8 million over five years](https://origin.arpa-h.gov/news-and-events/arpa-h-awards-teams-set-bioprint-universally-matched-organs-demand) through its Personalized Regenerative Immunocompetent Nanotechnology Tissue (PRINT) program. The goal is to develop the capability to 3D bioprint functional, personalized human organs —...

recommendations: Consider adding arpa-h.gov and university newsroom domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive article covering the ARPA-H PRINT program with detailed breakdowns of all five teams, their approaches, and funding amounts. Well-organized with clear sections. 801 words appropriate for News category.
editor_notes.source_verification: Verified 4 of 7 sources directly. ARPA-H confirmed: $176.8M total, 5 teams, PRINT program, Jan 12 announcement, Alicia Jackson quote. Wake Forest confirmed: $24.8M, Atala/Yoo, Spitler quote verbatim, 120K waitlist/45K transplants, partners list. UT Southwestern confirmed: $25M, Rizwan, VITAL project, iPSCs/hydrogel, 10-13 weeks, 5-year timeline. UC San Diego confirmed: $25.8M, Chen, light-based approach, AI vascular design, Allele Biotech, 'holy grail' quote verbatim. Rice and EurekAlert sources not fetched but are supplementary to already-verified claims.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major claims verified. Title says $177M vs actual $176.8M — acceptable rounding for headline. Award amounts, team leaders, quotes, technical approaches, and transplant statistics all match sources precisely. No hallucinated details detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and informative. Appropriately notes the aggressive timeline and remaining technical hurdles without dismissing the program's ambition. Good balance.
editor_notes.originality: No prior ARPA-H PRINT program coverage in the archive. Novel topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent, thoroughly sourced article with 7 sources including primary institutional announcements from all major teams. All key claims verified. Ready for publication.
